title: Thousand Pillar Temple
tagline: Thousand Pillar Temple — spiritual town of Telangana

overview.short: The Thousand Pillar Temple or Rudreswara Swamy Temple is a historical Hindu temple located in the city of Hanamakonda, Telangana, India.
overview.description: The Thousand Pillar Temple or Rudreswara Swamy Temple is a historical Hindu temple located in the city of Hanamakonda, Telangana, India. It is dedicated to Shiva, Vishnu and Surya. The Thousand Pillar Temple, along with the Warangal Fort and the Kakatiya Kala Thoranam were added to the tentative list of UNESCO World Heritage sites.

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Padmakshi Temple
topPlaces.description: Padmakshi Temple is one of the oldest temples in the Hanamakonda area of Telangana, India. It is dedicated to the Hindu goddess Padmakshi (Lakshmi), and also features Jain imagery.

topPlaces.name: Bhadrakali Lake
topPlaces.description: Bhadrakali Lake is a lake in Warangal, Telangana built by Ganapati of Kakatiya dynasty. The lake is situated near the famous Bhadrakali Temple.

topPlaces.name: Bhadrakali Temple, Warangal
topPlaces.description: Bhadrakali Temple is one of the oldest temples for Goddess Bhadrakali, situated on a hilltop in the city of Warangal, Telangana, India.

topPlaces.name: Public Garden, Hanamkonda
topPlaces.description: Public Garden is a public park located in Hanamkonda, Telangana. Park was developed and created by Collector S. M. Naimuddin Sahib on 19th occasion as per tablet found in park building.

topPlaces.name: Warangal Museum
topPlaces.description: Warangal ASI Museum is a museum located in the musical garden complex in Warangal, Telangana, India. It is maintained by Archaeological Survey of India.

topPlaces.name: Kakatiya Musical Garden
topPlaces.description: Kakatiya Musical Garden is a musical garden located near the Bhadrakali temple in Warangal, Telangana. The garden was established in 1994 and it covers an area of 18 acres (73,000 m2).

topPlaces.name: Kakatiya Zoological Park
topPlaces.description: Kakatiya Zoological Park (also known as Warangal Vana Vignana Kendram or Warangal Zoo) is a zoo situated in Hanamakonda, Hanamkonda district of Telangana, India.

topPlaces.name: Late 2011 Telangana protests
topPlaces.description: The late 2011 Telangana protests refers to a chain of protests as part of Telangana movement between September and December 2011. Sakala Janula samme or All Peoples Strike is the biggest protest of all.

items.note: Padmakshi Temple is one of the oldest temples in the Hanamakonda area of Telangana, India. It is dedicated to the Hindu goddess Padmakshi (Lakshmi), and also features Jain imagery.
Bhadrakali Lake is a lake in Warangal, Telangana built by Ganapati of Kakatiya dynasty. The lake is situated near the famous Bhadrakali Temple.
Bhadrakali Temple is one of the oldest temples for Goddess Bhadrakali, situated on a hilltop in the city of Warangal, Telangana, India.
Public Garden is a public park located in Hanamkonda, Telangana. Park was developed and created by Collector S. M. Naimuddin Sahib on 19th occasion as per tablet found in park building.
Warangal ASI Museum is a museum located in the musical garden complex in Warangal, Telangana, India. It is maintained by Archaeological Survey of India.
Kakatiya Musical Garden is a musical garden located near the Bhadrakali temple in Warangal, Telangana. The garden was established in 1994 and it covers an area of 18 acres (73,000 m2).
Kakatiya Zoological Park (also known as Warangal Vana Vignana Kendram or Warangal Zoo) is a zoo situated in Hanamakonda, Hanamkonda district of Telangana, India.

The late 2011 Telangana protests refers to a chain of protests as part of Telangana movement between September and December 2011. Sakala Janula samme or All Peoples Strike is the biggest protest of all.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Thousand Pillar Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Thousand Pillar Temple is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Thousand Pillar Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Rajiv Gandhi International Airport, Hyderabad (148 km) and the nearest railway station is Secunderabad Junction (Hyderabad) (130 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Thousand Pillar Temple?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Thousand Pillar Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Thousand Pillar Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Thousand Pillar Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Thousand Pillar Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Padmakshi Temple, Bhadrakali Lake, Bhadrakali Temple, Warangal, Public Garden, Hanamkonda, Warangal Museum.
